data.body:
## Context

CI currently fails on every push because 3 RED-marked tests (TDD placeholders for incomplete v2→v3 migration work) cause the Script Tests job to exit non-zero. These are known false-positives that don't impact production — the pre-commit test gate already tolerates them via `.test-index` markers. But `suite-engine.sh` has no exclusion mechanism, so CI reports failure, masking real regressions and eroding trust in the build signal.

## Success Criteria

- `suite-engine.sh` accepts an optional `--exclude-file <path>` flag; tests listed in that file are skipped with a `SKIP (excluded)` status line
- When `--exclude-file` is not passed, suite-engine behavior is identical to today (no behavioral change for existing callers)
- CI generates the exclusion list from `.test-index` RED markers before invoking test runners, so RED-marked tests never run in CI
- CI Script Tests job passes when the only "failures" are RED-marked tests
- Skipped tests are counted and reported in the suite summary (e.g., `PASSED: 178  FAILED: 0  SKIPPED: 3`) so their existence remains visible

## Dependencies

None. RED markers already exist in `.test-index` from the v2→v3 migration (epic d775-4a36, in progress). This epic consumes existing markers; it does not require d775-4a36 to complete first. When d775-4a36 eventually removes the RED markers, the exclusion list becomes empty and CI behavior is unchanged (no tests skipped).

## Approach

Add an `--exclude-file` flag to `suite-engine.sh` that accepts a file of test paths to skip. CI workflow generates this list by parsing `.test-index` for RED markers (`[marker]` entries) before invoking test runners. Backward-compatible: flag is optional, behavior unchanged when omitted.
